study.title: OpenfMRI ds000115 working-memory task data
study.population: Schizophrenia, unaffected sibling, control sibling, and healthy control participants from ds000115.
study.design: Open working-memory dataset with behavioural participant summary columns.
study.notes: Candidate imported summary from participants.tsv. Event files are not used because the dataset page warns against relying on them.

samples.label: OpenfMRI ds000115 working-memory participants
samples.population: Schizophrenia, unaffected sibling, control sibling, and healthy control participants from ds000115.
samples.exclusionCriteria: Metric-level exclusions apply where behavioural summary columns are missing or invalid.
samples.notes: Split diagnostic groups before making cohort-similarity or clinical-context claims.

samples.label: OpenfMRI ds000115 healthy controls
samples.population: Healthy control participants from ds000115 with condit=CON.
samples.inclusionCriteria: Participants represented in ds000115_R2.0.0 participants.tsv with condit=CON.
samples.exclusionCriteria: Metric-level exclusions apply where behavioural summary columns are missing or invalid.
samples.notes: Healthy-control subgroup used for the first public task-specific reference comparison.

tasks.label: 2-back working-memory task
tasks.taskVariant: Letter 2-back behavioural summary columns from participants.tsv.
tasks.notes: Boundary compatibility review must verify task timing, stimulus type, and load.

measures.label: Accuracy
measures.transformation: Subject-level 2-back accuracy is the unweighted mean of nback2_nont and nback2_targ because trial counts are not represented in participants.tsv.
measures.notes: Candidate 2-back accuracy summary derived from participants.tsv target and non-target accuracy columns.

measures.label: Healthy-control accuracy
measures.transformation: Filtered to condit=CON. Subject-level 2-back accuracy is the unweighted mean of nback2_nont and nback2_targ because trial counts are not represented in participants.tsv.
measures.notes: Reviewed healthy-control 2-back accuracy summary from participants.tsv for task-specific reference context.

measures.label: Sensitivity d'
measures.transformation: Summary of d4prime values in participants.tsv after removing missing values and one out-of-range row.
measures.notes: Candidate sensitivity summary from the source d4prime column; validate that d4prime aligns with Boundary's signal-detection definition before public comparisons.

results.notes: Candidate 2-back accuracy summary derived from participants.tsv target and non-target accuracy columns.
Reviewed healthy-control 2-back accuracy summary derived from participants.tsv target and non-target accuracy columns.
Candidate sensitivity summary from the source d4prime column; validate that d4prime aligns with Boundary's signal-detection definition before public comparisons.

comparisonClaims.claim: Boundary n-back accuracy can be compared with the imported 2-back accuracy distribution only after task-variant compatibility is reviewed.
comparisonClaims.guardrail: Do not present this as a diagnosis, ADHD marker, or public percentile until the dataset is validated.

comparisonClaims.claim: This run's n-back accuracy can be compared with the OpenfMRI ds000115 healthy-control 2-back accuracy distribution as task-specific reference context.
comparisonClaims.guardrail: Similarity to this healthy-control reference distribution is not a diagnosis, trait label, or clinical classification; the source used a letter 2-back summary and Boundary uses its own short task variant.

comparisonClaims.claim: Boundary n-back d' can be compared with the imported d4prime distribution only after the source metric is aligned with Boundary's signal-detection calculation.
comparisonClaims.guardrail: Use this for internal review and smoke tests, not public cohort-similarity language.

guardrails: The OpenfMRI dataset page warns that event files may be inaccurate. This summary uses participants.tsv behavioural columns rather than event TSV reconstruction.
Healthy-control 2-back accuracy is reviewed for task-specific reference context; other metrics and diagnostic subgroups remain candidate-only.
The sample mixes diagnostic and control groups; split cohorts before resemblance claims.
reviewerNotes: First structured extraction seed. Healthy-control accuracy is reviewed for a guarded participant-facing reference comparison; sensitivity and mixed-diagnostic claims remain candidate-only.
